summary: Alfred is a well-structured Rust text editor with strong architectural boundaries, comprehensive testing (183 unit tests), 6 documented ADRs, and a plugin-first design where all user-facing features are Lisp plugins. The functional-core/imperative-shell pattern is cleanly enforced by 5 Cargo crates. Main areas for improvement: inline documentation density and the concentration of logic in app.rs (7,449 lines).

documentation_coverage.notes: Every public function and type has a doc comment. Six ADRs cover all major decisions. A comprehensive vim commands research document exists. CLAUDE.md serves as the project README.

architecture_clarity.notes: All dependencies point inward toward alfred-core. No circular dependencies. Naming is consistent: kebab-case for commands, snake_case for Rust, module::function style for pure functions.

top_hotspots.notes: Contains event loop, all input states (13 variants), operator-pending logic, colon commands, search, visual mode, macro replay, and more. This is the highest-risk file -- both the most complex and most frequently changed.
EditorState struct has 30+ fields. Contains builtin command registration (80+ commands). Growing as new vim features are added.
All bridge primitives. Each new Lisp primitive adds a function here. Pattern is consistent but file is growing.

risk_details.description: God-file risk. app.rs is 7,449 lines with 13 InputState variants handled in a single function. Extract operator-pending, visual mode, colon commands, and search into separate modules.
EditorState struct has 30+ fields. Consider grouping related fields into sub-structs (e.g., SearchState, MacroState, VisualState) to improve readability.
Growing bridge file. Consider splitting into bridge/buffer.rs, bridge/keymap.rs, bridge/hooks.rs, bridge/theme.rs for better organization.

recommendations.title: Extract app.rs into sub-modules
recommendations.description: Split crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs (7,449 lines) into focused modules: operator_pending.rs, visual_mode.rs, colon_commands.rs, search.rs, macro_replay.rs. The handle_key_event function is currently 500+ lines with 13 match arms.

recommendations.title: Group EditorState fields into sub-structs
recommendations.description: EditorState has 30+ fields. Group them: SearchState (pattern, forward, last_char_find), MacroState (registers, recording, buffer, replaying), VisualState (selection_start, visual_line_mode), JumpState (jump_list, jump_index, change_list, change_list_index).

recommendations.title: Split bridge.rs into category modules
recommendations.description: The bridge module registers 20+ primitives in one file. Split into bridge/buffer.rs, bridge/cursor.rs, bridge/keymap.rs, bridge/hooks.rs, bridge/theme.rs for easier navigation.

recommendations.title: Add doc-tests for Lisp primitives
recommendations.description: Bridge primitives have doc comments describing usage but no doc-tests. Adding runnable examples in doc comments would catch regressions and serve as living documentation.

recommendations.title: Add property-based tests for cursor and buffer operations
recommendations.description: CLAUDE.md specifies property-based testing as the default strategy, but no proptest/quickcheck tests exist yet. Cursor movement functions (pure, well-defined domains) are ideal candidates.
